source: Smelling Salts Review — extracted from conversation history line 82
name: Service Naming Convention — Ruby's Voice
description: All 200+ Maven services must be named with character — sassy, urgent, warm, authentic, vulnerable. Names that sound like Ruby talking to her neighbor over the fence, not a corporate brochure. The Named Innovation Library has been created with character names for all Big Mama innovations. The remaining services need the same treatment.

naming_principles.voice: Ruby Red's voice — authentic, transparent, vulnerable
naming_principles.tone: Sassy when possible, fun, talkative, urgent, with a sense of urgency
naming_principles.test: Would a 35-45 year old working-poor mom smile, feel seen, or feel hopeful hearing this name? If yes — it passes.

named_examples_from_vault.tagline: Twenty-five bucks. No interest. No shame. Just help.
If we can guarantee TP, we can guarantee anything.
Those subscriptions are bleeding you dry. Let's fix that.

named_examples_from_vault.original: I Got a Guy Agent Network
named_examples_from_vault.tagline: Grace always knows someone who knows someone.
You win half. You pick who gets the rest.
Your good name, tracked and rewarded.

named_examples_from_vault.ruby_name: The Good Side of the Feed
named_examples_from_vault.tagline: Community talk without the drama. Finally.
This week, someone's fridge is full. Could be yours.
Fifty bucks on the tank. Get to work, mama.

what_still_needs_naming: The Named Innovation Library covers Big Mama innovations. The remaining 143 Maven Tier services need the same character-driven naming treatment.
